title: Structure and Function of Blood Vessels

questions.text: Which layer of a blood vessel is primarily responsible for regulating lumen diameter through vasoconstriction and vasodilation?
What is the function of the internal elastic lamina found in blood vessel walls?

answers.text: To anchor the vessel to surrounding tissues
To facilitate diffusion through window-like openings
To generate electrical impulses for vasoconstriction
A thick tunica media dominated by smooth muscle
A thick tunica media dominated by elastic fibers
The ability to distribute blood to specific organs
Greater capacity for vasoconstriction than muscular arteries

questions.text: The ability of an anastomosis to provide collateral circulation is most important when:

answers.text: A blood vessel becomes blocked or damaged

questions.text: What controls blood flow into specific capillary beds?
Which blood vessels are the most distensible elements of the vascular system and serve as a reservoir for large volumes of blood?
What prevents blood from flowing backward in veins?

answers.text: The elastic recoil of the vessel wall
High pressure in the venous system

questions.text: At rest, the largest portion of blood volume is found in:
During hemorrhage, vasoconstriction of which vessels helps redistribute blood to maintain survivable blood pressure?
A nurse notices that when a patient's skin is cut, bleeding initially stops quickly. This is primarily due to:

answers.text: A vascular spasm of the tunica media
